channel.title: The Sentinel Review — National Security Feed
channel.description: Automated national security news aggregation. Updated every 8 hours.

item.title: 🌏 Quad nations step up Indo-Pacific push with new initiatives | Defense News
item.description: The announcement to build port infrastructure in Fiji comes amid China’s deepening ties with the South Pacific island, which straddles vital sea lanes.

item.title: 🏴 At French Army FPV competition, NATO soldiers race drones and swap tips | Defense News
item.description: “When I put on the mask, I no longer exist in this reality,” Chief Cpl. Clément said after completing the course.

item.title: 🔐 How America Lost Its Most Important Defense Tech Habit | War on the Rocks
item.description: On April 15, technology podcaster Dwarkesh Patel published a two-hour interview with Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang. For roughly forty minutes, Patel asked one question six different ways. The question was this: If American-made compute trains AI models with the serious cyber-offensive…

item.title: 🌏 Contriving Imaginary Gaps in Nuclear Deterrence | War on the Rocks
item.description: Among some nuclear strategists, military officers, and lawmakers, a belief bordering on the canonical has taken root that the United States is on the short end of a “deterrence gap” with Russia and China. Both countries, and especially Russia, possess theater-range nuclear…

item.title: 📍 Glass Jaw? The New Economic Fragility Recasting American Power | War on the Rocks
item.description: A pair of children’s shoes is an odd place to look for the changing dynamics of American power. But stick with me because, after the past year, it is one of the clearest places to see them.Long before those shoes reach a store shelf, tariffs have raised the cost of materials,…

item.title: 🌍 Why Iran will prefer ongoing conflict | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Even a fragile ceasefire creates the illusion that a resolution pathway is in progress. In such highly contentious regions as the Middle East, ceasefires frequently function as transitional phases between confrontation, rather than endpoints. The key ...

item.title: 🌏 Don’t Count the Quad Out | Foreign Policy
item.description: A more modest agenda is also more credible.

item.title: 🌏 China’s AI Heist | Foreign Affairs
item.description: How to counter Beijing’s unauthorized "distillation."

item.title: 📍 Cuba’s Only Option | Foreign Affairs
item.description: A deal with Washington is the island’s best hope.

item.title: 🏴 How NATO can regain initiative on its eastern flank | ASPI Strategist
item.description: While NATO can still deter Russian aggression with Article 5, it is losing the initiative on its eastern flank in the grey zone between peace and war. The problem is not military weakness, but a ...

item.title: 🏛️ MQ-28 Ghost Bat Now Flying Over The Pacific From U.S. Navy Base | The War Zone
item.description: Boeing says tests off California are about showing the Australian-born MQ-28's maturity and promoting sales, which could include ones to the Pentagon. The post MQ-28 Ghost Bat Now Flying Over The Pacific From U.S. Navy Base appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏴 Ukraine’s legacy grid and wartime agility could help answer Europe’s energy problem | Atlantic Council
item.description: Ukraine’s strong position as a potential supplier of European energy is rooted in its resilience, resources, and resolve. The post Ukraine’s legacy grid and wartime agility could help answer Europe’s energy problem appeared first on Atlantic Council.

item.title: 🌍 Groundhog Day: Iran War Edition | Foreign Policy
item.description: It’s an open question if negotiators can break the repetitive cycle engulfing the war.

item.title: 🌏 As China’s surpluses become unbearable, the EU is edging toward its own Section 301 | Atlantic Council
item.description: As China’s industrial surpluses flood global markets, the EU is quietly debating whether it needs its own version of US Section 301—marking a potential shift toward more assertive trade defense tools against Beijing. The post As China’s surpluses become unbearable, the EU is…

item.title: 🌍 Tentative U.S.-Iran Cease-Fire Extension Deal Awaits Trump’s Approval | Foreign Policy
item.description: Tehran’s leadership has also not publicly signed off on the agreement yet.

item.title: 🌍 No Commodity Is Safe From the Iran War | Foreign Policy
item.description: From Diet Coke to condoms, the world’s supply chains have faced surprising downstream disruptions.

item.title: 🏴 New Counter-Drone Optimized Pantsir Air Defense System Being Deployed Atop Skyscrapers In Moscow | The War Zone
item.description: A video showing a Pantsir-SMD-E being airlifted onto a skyscraper underscores Russian attempts to shield Moscow from increasing Ukrainian drone attacks. The post New Counter-Drone Optimized Pantsir Air Defense System Being Deployed Atop Skyscrapers In Moscow appeared first on…

item.title: 🌏 A framework for US-Japan cooperation in the Arctic | Atlantic Council
item.description: Japan's status as a maritime state and proximity to the Arctic Ocean means it is easily affected by climate-driven environmental shifts, evolving maritime routes, and infrastructure changes in the Arctic. How should it approach the rapidly changing region? The post A framework…

item.title: 📍 Security institutions need to take care seriously | ASPI Strategist
item.description: Across global security institutions, caring responsibilities are reshaping who can deploy, who can lead and who remains in uniform. New research from Monash University’s Global Peace and Security research hub, funded by Global Affairs Canada ...

item.title: 🏛️ Army’s data-merging cell needs a few years to untangle the mess | Defense One
item.description: The pilot program runs through September, after which the Army will need to officially fund and staff it to keep it going.

item.title: 📍 Epic Fury highlighted Space Force needs for distributed ops, EW sites | Breaking Defense
item.description: “We’ve seen in Operation Epic Fury, for the first time, that our space capabilities have been targeted and destroyed. We expect that to happen more,” Brig. Gen. Christopher Fernengel said today.

item.title: 🌍 Iran war needs an inspector general, senator notes | Defense One
item.description: The law says an IG must be appointed when an “overseas contingency operation” surpasses 60 days.

item.title: 🌏 Hollow Deals, Tricky Negotiations, and State Visits | War on the Rocks
item.description: Welcome to The Adversarial. Every other week, we’ll provide you with expert analysis on America’s greatest challengers: China, Russia, Iran, North Korea, and jihadists. Read more below.***IranThere has been increasing chatter that Washington and Tehran are nearing an agreement…

item.title: ⚛️ BAE Systems wins Army’s Soft Kill APS award, with first phase valued at $20 million | Breaking Defense
item.description: The system uses BAE’s Rapid Optical Observation and Kill (ROOK) program, which takes down threats by “confusing,” or jamming missile systems or drones.

item.title: 🏛️ Army Wants More Sensor-Laden Surveillance Balloons Over The Pacific | The War Zone
item.description: High-altitude balloons are an increasingly critical part of the Army's future combat strategy, from providing networking to sensing to delivering kinetic effects. The post Army Wants More Sensor-Laden Surveillance Balloons Over The Pacific appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🏛️ US Army turns resupply drone into rocket launcher in new test | Defense News
item.description: Though the drone was designed to haul supplies across the battlefield, the Army recently tried turning it into a rocket launcher.

item.title: 🔐 Severity Of America’s Depleted Advanced Weapons Stockpiles Detailed In New Report | The War Zone
item.description: America's stockpile of critical standoff and air and missile defense weapons is now a strategic vulnerability foes could take advantage of. The post Severity Of America’s Depleted Advanced Weapons Stockpiles Detailed In New Report appeared first on The War Zone.

item.title: 🌍 The Iran War Has Only Worsened Sudan’s Conflict | Foreign Policy
item.description: The country is uniquely vulnerable to regional shocks.

item.title: 📍 Turkey’s defense industry is leaning into autonomy and targeting Gulf markets | Breaking Defense
item.description: Middle East Bureau Chief Agnes Helou shares what she learned at Turkey’s SAHA defense expo.

item.title: 🏛️ The Navy used drones to sink a retired warship | Defense One
item.description: Lessons from the SINKEX are shaping the service’s plans to buy and fight.
